red_symptom: exact_max_size_with_newline_succeeds panicked with InvalidMessage("response too large: 10485761 bytes (max 10485760)") — off-by-one in post-allocation guard rejected at-limit messages; oversized-no-newline test passed pre-fix only because BufReader could allocate the 100MB locally inside the 5s deadline (symptom-disguised; the size bound is now enforced before allocation rather than after).
post_fix_invariant: IpcStream::recv_raw wraps the inner stream in tokio::io::AsyncReadExt::take(stream, MAX_MESSAGE_SIZE + 1) before BufReader::read_line, so any payload exceeding MAX_MESSAGE_SIZE is rejected BEFORE allocation can grow unbounded. Both Unix (UnixStream) and Windows (TcpStream) arms delegate to a shared recv_raw_from<R: AsyncRead + Unpin> helper, structurally guaranteeing the invariant on both platforms.
